competencies.competency_text: Describes the relationship between the natural environment and cultural practices in their immediate environment (nature of work, food, festivals, traditions)
competencies.justification: Every named section in the chapter is built around the nature-culture relationship: rosy starlings (natural migration) helping Indian farmers (cultural practice); yoga (cultural tradition) rooted in harmony with nature; chilli (natural plant) adopted into Indian cuisine (cultural practice); marigold (natural flower from Mexico) embedded in Indian festivals; Indian cow breeds (natural animal resource) sustaining Brazilian dairy culture; and the 'Web of Life' synthesis explicitly states that people, animals, plants and ideas are woven together. This is the chapter's thematic spine.
sections: The Blue Planet
Story 1: The Travelling Birds!
Story 2: Yoga—India's Gift to the World!
Story 3: Chilli—A Spice that Changed our Lives!
Story 4: The Sweet Story of Sugar!
Story 5: The Mexican Marigold Moves into India!
Story 6: The Cows that Went to Brazil!
Web of Life
One Earth, One Family!

competencies.competency_text: Connects changes in the environment and the lives of their family and community, as communicated by elders and through local stories (changes in occupation, food habits, resources, celebrations, communication)
competencies.justification: Story 3 (Chilli) describes how food habits changed when chilli arrived in India 400–500 years ago and the ask-your-parents task directly connects the student with elders to trace this food-habit change. Story 4 (Sugar) similarly describes how sugar production and spread changed diets globally. Activity 5 in Web of Life explicitly asks students to interview a grandparent or neighbour about a food, song, or custom that was absent in their childhood but is common today — a direct C-2.3 operation of connecting change communicated by elders.
sections: Story 3: Chilli—A Spice that Changed our Lives!
Story 4: The Sweet Story of Sugar!
Web of Life

competencies.competency_text: Observes and describes cultural diversity in their immediate environment (food, clothing, games, different seasons, festivals related to harvest and sowing)
competencies.justification: Story 2 (Yoga) asks students to discuss why yoga became popular globally and to name a yoga pose — engaging with a cultural practice observed across countries. Story 3 (Chilli) and Story 4 (Sugar) describe cultural diversity in food habits (chilli in Indian vs. South American cuisine; sugar-making originating in India). Story 5 (Marigold) asks students to write why both Indian and Mexican cultures use marigolds in their festivals and why people in different countries include flowers in celebrations — directly describing cultural diversity around festivals.
sections: Story 2: Yoga—India's Gift to the World!
Story 3: Chilli—A Spice that Changed our Lives!
Story 5: The Mexican Marigold Moves into India!

competencies.competency_text: Learns about basic social and behavioural norms, values, and dispositions that benefit our social and natural environments and that help our society function smoothly (using dustbins, standing in queues, conserving water, using public transportation, keeping one's environment clean, always helping others in need regardless of background)
competencies.justification: The 'One Earth, One Family!' section teaches Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam ('the world is one family') as a social-ethical disposition — students are required to write what this teaches, express it in their own words, and articulate how to live as one family despite differences. 'Let us reflect' tasks ask students to write one way to care for the Earth. The chapter's closing message that Earth needs care and that caring for it means caring for each other is an architectural instantiation of the social-behavioural dispositions C-4.7 defines.
sections: One Earth, One Family!

competencies.competency_text: Reads simple maps of city, state, and country to identify natural and humanmade features (well, lake, post office, school, hospital) with reference to symbols and directions
competencies.justification: Activity 1 (The Blue Planet) instructs students to use a globe to find whether all oceans are connected and to locate India — a direct globe/map reading operation to identify natural (oceans) and geographic (India) features. Activity 2 (Story 1) asks students to trace rosy starling migration routes from Russia/Mongolia to India on a globe using a string. Activity 3 (Story 3) instructs students to trace the chilli journey from South America to India on a globe. Activity 5 (Web of Life) asks students to use two coloured strings to trace journeys to and from India on a map.
sections: The Blue Planet
Story 1: The Travelling Birds!
Story 3: Chilli—A Spice that Changed our Lives!
Web of Life

competencies.competency_text: Performs simple inquiry related to specific questions independently or in groups
competencies.justification: Activity 2 item 1 (Story 1) asks students to find out where 5 winter birds come from. Activity 3 item 2 (Story 3) asks students to research and write the travel story of one food (potato, tomato, peanut, or cashew). Activity 4 item 1 (Story 6) asks students to find out at least 5 breeds of cows with help from a teacher or elder. Activity 5 item 1 (Web of Life) asks students to interview a grandparent or neighbour about a cultural change in their lifetime. Each is a structured independent or guided inquiry task answering a specific question.
sections: Story 1: The Travelling Birds!
